name: TORC1 inhibition reduced infections and improved vaccine response in phase 2a
summary: A 264-participant older-adult phase 2a trial reported a functional respiratory-infection signal alongside antiviral gene-expression and influenza-vaccine-response movement.
statement: In a phase 2a randomized placebo-controlled trial of 264 older adults, six weeks of low-dose TORC1 inhibition was reported as safe and was associated with a significant decrease in infection rate over the following year, upregulated antiviral gene expression, and improved influenza-vaccine response.
time_horizon: Six weeks of treatment with infection follow-up over about one year
quantitative_note: The PubMed abstract reports a significant infection-rate decrease with P=0.001.
caveats: This is a phase 2a signal and depends on dose, schedule, and drug combination.
The finding belongs to the mTOR/TORC1 branch of immune intervention rather than a general immune-rejuvenation class effect.
Later RTB101 phase 3 respiratory-illness testing did not replicate the clinical endpoint.
